test_cases.test_case: Successful login with valid credentials redirects to Dashboard
steps: 1. Open the Login page
2. Enter <valid username> in the Username field
3. Enter <valid password> in the Password field
4. Click the "Log in" button
test_cases.expected_result: redirects student to Dashboard

test_cases.test_case: Access the site as a guest via Access as guest button
steps: 1. Open the Login page
2. Click the "Access as a guest" button
test_cases.expected_result: grants guest access for unauthenticated browsing

test_cases.test_case: Open Cookies notice to view cookie usage information
steps: 1. Open the Login page
2. Click the "Cookies notice" button
test_cases.expected_result: displays cookie usage information

test_cases.test_case: Verify 'Lost password?' link is present but disabled on the Login page
steps: 1. Open the Login page
test_cases.expected_result: The 'Lost password?' link is visible and disabled on the Login page

test_cases.test_case: Submit with Username blank (text required field representative)
preconditions: User is unauthenticated and on the Login page
steps: 1. Leave the Username field blank
2. Enter <valid password> in the Password field
3. Click the 'Log in' button
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error appears on the Username field indicating it is required; the form does not submit; user remains on the Login page; Password field retains the entered value until the form blocks submission (Password is not cleared because failure is client-side required-field validation).

test_cases.test_case: Submit with Password blank (password required field representative)
preconditions: User is unauthenticated and on the Login page
steps: 1. Enter <valid username> in the Username field
2. Leave the Password field blank
3. Click the 'Log in' button
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error appears on the Password field indicating it is required; the form does not submit; user remains on the Login page; Username value is retained.

test_cases.test_case: Submit with ALL required fields empty
preconditions: User is unauthenticated and on the Login page
steps: 1. Leave the Username field blank
2. Leave the Password field blank
3. Click the 'Log in' button
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation errors appear on both Username and Password fields indicating they are required; the form does not submit; user remains on the Login page.

test_cases.test_case: Submit with invalid credentials (authentication failure)
preconditions: User is unauthenticated and on the Login page
steps: 1. Enter <existing username or any username> in the Username field
2. Enter <incorrect password> in the Password field
3. Click the 'Log in' button
test_cases.expected_result: Authentication fails and an inline error message is shown near the form indicating the credentials are invalid; the Password field is cleared; the Username field retains the entered value; the user remains on the Login page (no redirect to Dashboard).

test_cases.test_case: Click the disabled 'Lost password?' link — no navigation
preconditions: User is unauthenticated and on the Login page
steps: 1. Attempt to click the 'Lost password?' link
test_cases.expected_result: The 'Lost password?' link is disabled/unavailable; clicking it does not navigate away or open a password recovery page; the user remains on the Login page and no recovery UI is shown.

test_cases.test_case: Very long Username is retained after failed login
steps: 1. Focus the Username field
2. Enter a very long string (200+ characters) in the Username field
3. Focus the Password field
4. Enter an incorrect password in the Password field
5. Click the Log in button
test_cases.expected_result: Login is blocked; an inline error message is shown. The Password field is cleared. The Username field retains the exact very long string entered (no client-side truncation visible).

test_cases.test_case: Username with leading and trailing whitespace is preserved after failed login
steps: 1. Focus the Username field
2. Enter a Username value that includes leading and trailing whitespace
3. Focus the Password field
4. Enter an incorrect password in the Password field
5. Click the Log in button
test_cases.expected_result: Login is blocked; an inline error message is shown. The Password field is cleared. The Username field retains the exact value entered including leading and trailing whitespace (visible in the Username input).

test_cases.test_case: Rapid double submission of Log in leads to a single failure response and cleared password
steps: 1. Enter an existing Username in the Username field
2. Enter an incorrect password in the Password field
3. Click the Log in button
4. Immediately click the Log in button again (second click occurs before UI response completes)
test_cases.expected_result: Second submission attempt is blocked / ignored; only a single inline error message is shown. The Password field is cleared once. The Username field remains populated. No redirect to Dashboard occurs.

test_cases.test_case: Disabled 'Lost password?' link does not navigate
steps: 1. Locate the 'Lost password?' link which is disabled
2. Click the disabled 'Lost password?' link
test_cases.expected_result: Click is blocked; no navigation occurs and no new page or modal is opened. The login form remains displayed unchanged and no error message is shown.

test_cases.test_case: Enable Edit Mode shows edit controls on blocks
steps: 1. Click the Edit Mode checkbox to enable Edit Mode
test_cases.expected_result: toggles dashboard edit mode; edit controls become visible or hidden — the Edit Mode checkbox is shown as enabled and both the Move handle and the Block menu are visible on the Timeline and Calendar blocks

test_cases.test_case: Open Add Block page while in Edit Mode
steps: 1. Click the '+ Add a block' button below the Dashboard heading
test_cases.expected_result: opens page listing all available block types

test_cases.test_case: Reset dashboard page to default in Edit Mode
steps: 1. Click the 'Reset page to default' button at the top right
2. If a confirmation dialog appears, click Confirm
test_cases.expected_result: resets page to default — the dashboard layout reverts to the default blocks and positions and any custom block placements are no longer present

test_cases.test_case: Use Timeline block move handle to reposition the block
steps: 1. Click and hold the Timeline block Move handle
2. Drag the Timeline block to <new position> on the dashboard and release
test_cases.expected_result: enables dragging to reposition the Timeline block — the Timeline block is shown in the new location on the dashboard

test_cases.test_case: Open Timeline block configuration from block menu
steps: 1. Click the Timeline block's three-dot Block menu
2. Click 'Configure' from the menu
test_cases.expected_result: opens block configuration for the Timeline block

test_cases.test_case: Reposition Timeline block via block menu Move action
steps: 1. Click the Timeline block's three-dot Block menu
2. Click 'Move' from the menu
3. Use the shown drag controls to move the Timeline block to <new position> and release
test_cases.expected_result: enables repositioning of the Timeline block via the menu — a drag overlay/handle appears and the Timeline block is shown in the new position on the dashboard

test_cases.test_case: Delete Timeline block via block menu
steps: 1. Click the Timeline block's three-dot Block menu
2. Click 'Delete' from the menu
3. Click Confirm on the Delete dialog
test_cases.expected_result: The Timeline block is no longer visible on the dashboard

test_cases.test_case: Open new personal event form from Calendar block
steps: 1. Click the Calendar block 'New event' button
test_cases.expected_result: opens new personal event form

test_cases.test_case: Navigate calendar to previous month
preconditions: Calendar block is present showing current month
steps: 1. Click the Calendar block 'Previous month' button
test_cases.expected_result: navigates to previous month — the Calendar heading updates to the previous month and year

test_cases.test_case: Navigate calendar to next month
preconditions: Calendar block is present showing current month
steps: 1. Click the Calendar block 'Next month' button
test_cases.expected_result: navigates to next month — the Calendar heading updates to the next month and year

test_cases.test_case: Open dedicated full calendar view
steps: 1. Click the 'Full calendar' link at the bottom of the Calendar block
test_cases.expected_result: opens dedicated calendar view

test_cases.test_case: Open calendar import/export management
steps: 1. Click the 'Import or export calendars' link at the bottom of the Calendar block
test_cases.expected_result: opens calendar data management

test_cases.test_case: Use Calendar block move handle to reposition the block
steps: 1. Click and hold the Calendar block Move handle
2. Drag the Calendar block to <new position> on the dashboard and release
test_cases.expected_result: enables dragging to reposition the Calendar block — the Calendar block is shown in the new location on the dashboard

test_cases.test_case: Open Calendar block configuration from block menu
steps: 1. Click the Calendar block's three-dot Block menu
2. Click 'Configure' from the menu
test_cases.expected_result: opens block configuration for the Calendar block

test_cases.test_case: Reposition Calendar block via block menu Move action
steps: 1. Click the Calendar block's three-dot Block menu
2. Click 'Move' from the menu
3. Use the shown drag controls to move the Calendar block to <new position> and release
test_cases.expected_result: enables repositioning of the Calendar block via the menu — a drag overlay/handle appears and the Calendar block is shown in the new position on the dashboard

test_cases.test_case: Delete Calendar block via block menu
steps: 1. Click the Calendar block's three-dot Block menu
2. Click 'Delete' from the menu
3. Click Confirm on the Delete dialog
test_cases.expected_result: The Calendar block is no longer visible on the dashboard

test_cases.test_case: Timeline empty state displays when no activities in selected range
steps: 1. Open the Timeline block Time Range dropdown
2. Select <time range with no activities> from the Time Range dropdown
3. (Optional) Enter <search term> in the Timeline Search field and press Enter
test_cases.expected_result: No activities in the selected range is visible within the Timeline block

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to open '+ Add a block' when Edit Mode is OFF
steps: 1. Verify the Dashboard page is visible
2. Verify the Edit Mode toggle is OFF
3. Attempt to click the '+ Add a block' button below the Dashboard heading
test_cases.expected_result: The '+ Add a block' button is not visible / not interactable; clicking is not possible and the page listing available block types is not opened; Dashboard remains unchanged

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to use 'Reset page to default' when Edit Mode is OFF
steps: 1. Open the Dashboard page
2. Ensure the Edit Mode toggle is OFF
3. Attempt to click the 'Reset page to default' button at the top-right
test_cases.expected_result: The 'Reset page to default' button is not visible / not interactable; clicking is not possible and the Dashboard is not reset to defaults

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to drag Timeline block using Move handle when Edit Mode is OFF
preconditions: Timeline block is present on the page
steps: 1. Open the Dashboard page
2. Ensure the Edit Mode toggle is OFF
3. Attempt to press-and-drag the Timeline block's move handle to reposition the block
test_cases.expected_result: Timeline block's Move handle is not visible / dragging cannot be initiated; no reposition occurs and block remains in its original location

test_cases.test_case: Attempt 'Move' action from Timeline block menu when Edit Mode is OFF
preconditions: Timeline block is present on the page
steps: 1. Open the Dashboard page
2. Ensure the Edit Mode toggle is OFF
3. Attempt to open the Timeline block's three-dot 'Block menu' and select the 'Move' action
test_cases.expected_result: The block's edit menu (three-dot 'Block menu') and its 'Move' action are not visible / not accessible while Edit Mode is OFF; the 'Move' action cannot be selected and the block is not repositioned

test_cases.test_case: Attempt 'Configure' from Timeline block menu when Edit Mode is OFF
preconditions: Timeline block is present on the page
steps: 1. Open the Dashboard page
2. Ensure the Edit Mode toggle is OFF
3. Attempt to open the Timeline block's three-dot 'Block menu' and click 'Configure'
test_cases.expected_result: The block's edit controls, including the 'Block menu' and the 'Configure' option, are not visible while Edit Mode is OFF; configuration cannot be opened and no configuration modal/page appears

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to drag Calendar block using Move handle when Edit Mode is OFF
preconditions: Calendar block is present on the page
steps: 1. Open the Dashboard page
2. Ensure the Edit Mode toggle is OFF
3. Attempt to press-and-drag the Calendar block's move handle to reposition the block
test_cases.expected_result: Calendar block's Move handle is not visible / dragging cannot be initiated; the Calendar block remains in its original location

test_cases.test_case: Attempt 'Configure' from Calendar block menu when Edit Mode is OFF
preconditions: Calendar block is present on the page
steps: 1. Open the Dashboard page
2. Ensure the Edit Mode toggle is OFF
3. Attempt to open the Calendar block's three-dot 'Block menu' and click 'Configure'
test_cases.expected_result: The Calendar block's edit controls and 'Block menu' are not visible while Edit Mode is OFF; the 'Configure' option cannot be accessed and no configuration view appears

test_cases.test_case: Attempt 'Delete' for Calendar block via menu when Edit Mode is OFF
preconditions: Calendar block is present on the page
steps: 1. Open the Dashboard page
2. Ensure the Edit Mode toggle is OFF
3. Attempt to open the Calendar block's three-dot 'Block menu' and select 'Delete'
test_cases.expected_result: The Calendar block's edit controls and 'Block menu' are not visible while Edit Mode is OFF; 'Delete' is not accessible and the Calendar block is not removed

test_cases.test_case: Timeline empty state displayed when selected time range yields exactly 0 activities
preconditions: User is signed in and on the Dashboard
Edit Mode can be either on or off
steps: 1. Open the Timeline block Time Range dropdown
2. Select a Time Range that (in test data) yields exactly 0 activities
3. Observe the Timeline block content area
test_cases.expected_result: The Timeline block displays the empty state message "No activities in the selected range"; the empty state is visible and content area shows no activity rows — action succeeds

test_cases.test_case: Timeline does not show empty state when selected time range yields exactly 1 activity
preconditions: User is signed in and on the Dashboard
There is test data with exactly 1 activity in a chosen time range
steps: 1. Open the Timeline block Time Range dropdown
2. Select a Time Range that (in test data) yields exactly 1 activity
3. Observe the Timeline block content area
test_cases.expected_result: The Timeline block does not display the empty state message; the single activity row is visible in the block — action succeeds

test_cases.test_case: Rapid toggle of Edit Mode results in stable final state with edit controls matching the last toggle
preconditions: User is signed in and on the Dashboard
Initial Edit Mode state known (on or off)
steps: 1. Click the Edit Mode toggle to switch its state
2. Immediately click the Edit Mode toggle again to switch the state back
3. Repeat rapid toggle sequence several times (at least 3 quick toggles)
4. Stop toggling and observe the Edit Mode toggle final position and the dashboard UI
test_cases.expected_result: Dashboard ends in the state shown by the final toggle; if final state is On, edit controls (+ Add a block, Reset page to default, block move handles and three-dot menus) are visible; if final state is Off, those edit controls are hidden — UI stabilizes and this final state succeeds

test_cases.test_case: Add a block button visible and opens block types page when Edit Mode is on
preconditions: User is signed in and on the Dashboard
steps: 1. Click the Edit Mode toggle to turn Edit Mode on
2. Locate and click the "+ Add a block" button
test_cases.expected_result: Clicking "+ Add a block" opens the page listing all available block types; the action succeeds and the block types page is displayed

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to use "+ Add a block" when Edit Mode is off is blocked (button not present)
preconditions: User is signed in and on the Dashboard
steps: 1. Look for the "+ Add a block" button below the Dashboard heading
test_cases.expected_result: The "+ Add a block" button is not present; any attempt to interact with it is blocked because the control is not visible — is blocked

test_cases.test_case: Timeline search accepts a very long string (200+ characters) and executes search
preconditions: User is signed in and on the Dashboard
steps: 1. Focus the Timeline Search field
2. Enter a very long search string (200+ characters)
3. Press Enter or click the Search action
test_cases.expected_result: The Search field accepts the very long string and the search executes; the entered string remains visible in the Search field and results area updates to show matching activities or the empty state if none match — search succeeds

test_cases.test_case: Timeline search with special characters and emoji is accepted and executed
preconditions: User is signed in and on the Dashboard
steps: 1. Focus the Timeline Search field
2. Enter a search string containing special characters and emoji
3. Press Enter or click the Search action
test_cases.expected_result: The Search field retains the special characters and emoji; the search executes and results update according to the trimmed/normalized query (matches shown or empty state if none) — action succeeds

test_cases.test_case: Open Timeline block menu then toggle Edit Mode off before selecting Move — menu Move action is blocked
preconditions: User is signed in and on the Dashboard
Timeline block three-dot menu is visible
steps: 1. Click the Timeline block three-dot menu to open it
2. While the menu is open, click the Edit Mode toggle to turn Edit Mode off
3. Attempt to click the Move action in the open three-dot menu
test_cases.expected_result: Selecting Move after Edit Mode is turned off is blocked; the Move action is disabled or not executed and no repositioning UI appears — action is blocked

test_cases.test_case: Navigate to previous month removes highlight from the current-date cell not in view
preconditions: User is signed in and on the Dashboard
Calendar block displays the current month with the current date highlighted
steps: 1. Click the Calendar block "Previous month" button
2. Observe the calendar grid and the highlighted date cell
test_cases.expected_result: After navigating to the previous month, the calendar no longer highlights the original current date (that date is not in the displayed month); the calendar view updates to the previous month successfully — action succeeds

test_cases.test_case: Apply Status filter shows only courses with that status
preconditions: User is enrolled in multiple courses with varying statuses
steps: 1. Navigate to the My Courses page
2. From the Status dropdown, select <status>
3. Observe the Courses grid
test_cases.expected_result: Courses grid displays only courses with status <status>; courses with other statuses are no longer visible

test_cases.test_case: Search filters courses by name or category
preconditions: User is enrolled in multiple courses whose names and categories differ
steps: 1. Navigate to the My Courses page
2. Enter <query> in the Search field
3. Press Enter or wait for search to apply
4. Observe the Courses grid
test_cases.expected_result: Courses grid displays only courses whose name or category matches <query>; unrelated courses are no longer visible

test_cases.test_case: Change Sort reorders the courses by the selected criterion
preconditions: User is enrolled in multiple courses with differing values relevant to sort criteria
steps: 1. Navigate to the My Courses page
2. From the Sort dropdown, select <sort criterion>
3. Observe the order of courses in the Courses grid
test_cases.expected_result: Courses grid is ordered by <sort criterion>; course ordering visibly reflects the selected sort criterion

test_cases.test_case: Switch layout to Card displays courses as cards
preconditions: User is enrolled in at least one course
steps: 1. Navigate to the My Courses page
2. From the Layout dropdown, select 'Card'
3. Observe the Courses grid
test_cases.expected_result: Course grid switches to Card layout; each course is shown as a card with banner image, clickable course name link, and category

test_cases.test_case: Switch layout to List displays courses in a list view
preconditions: User is enrolled in multiple courses
steps: 1. Navigate to the My Courses page
2. From the Layout dropdown, select 'List'
3. Observe the Courses grid
test_cases.expected_result: Course grid switches to List layout; courses are displayed in a list-style layout appropriate for the List option

test_cases.test_case: Switch layout to Summary displays condensed course entries
preconditions: User is enrolled in multiple courses
steps: 1. Navigate to the My Courses page
2. From the Layout dropdown, select 'Summary'
3. Observe the Courses grid
test_cases.expected_result: Course grid switches to Summary layout; courses are displayed in the condensed Summary view

test_cases.test_case: Click course name navigates to the course main page
preconditions: At least one course is visible in the My Courses grid
steps: 1. Navigate to the My Courses page
2. Click the Course Name link on the <target course> card
test_cases.expected_result: The course's main page is displayed for the clicked course (the course main page is visible)

test_cases.test_case: Star a course from the card menu pins it to top and marks as Starred
preconditions: At least two courses are visible in the My Courses grid
steps: 1. Navigate to the My Courses page
2. Click the three-dot Card Menu on the <target course> card
3. Select 'Star this course' from the menu
test_cases.expected_result: pins the course to the top of the list and marks it as Starred

test_cases.test_case: Remove a course from view hides it without unenrolling
preconditions: At least one course is visible in the My Courses grid
steps: 1. Navigate to the My Courses page
2. Click the three-dot Card Menu on the <target course> card
3. Select 'Remove from view' from the menu
test_cases.expected_result: hides the course from the user's view without unenrolling (sets status to Hidden)

test_cases.test_case: Access My Courses while unauthenticated
preconditions: User is signed out / not authenticated
steps: 1. Ensure the user is signed out
2. Navigate to the My Courses page URL (<my courses page URL>)
test_cases.expected_result: User is not shown the My Courses grid. The application redirects to the login page (login screen is displayed) and no course cards, filters, or toolbar controls are visible. Access to My Courses is blocked until the user authenticates.

test_cases.test_case: Open course main page URL directly while unauthenticated
preconditions: User is signed out / not authenticated
There exists a course the user could access when authenticated
steps: 1. Ensure the user is signed out
2. Navigate directly to a course main page URL (the URL the Course Name link would open) - <course main page URL>
test_cases.expected_result: Navigation is blocked: the application redirects to the login page (login screen is displayed) and the course main page content is not visible. The user is not able to view the course page without authenticating.

test_cases.test_case: Very long search query (200+ chars) in Search field
preconditions: User is authenticated and on the My Courses page
The Courses grid has at least one course loaded
steps: 1. Focus the Search field
2. Enter <very long search string (200+ characters)> into the Search field
3. Press Enter to apply the search
test_cases.expected_result: Search succeeds: the Search field retains the entire entered string; the Courses grid updates to show only courses that match the literal long query. If no courses match, the grid shows the page's empty-state indication (visible message or empty list) and no error banner is shown.

test_cases.test_case: Search query containing special characters, punctuation, and emoji
preconditions: User is authenticated and on the My Courses page
The Courses grid has at least one course loaded
steps: 1. Focus the Search field
2. Enter <search string containing special characters, punctuation, and emoji> into the Search field
3. Press Enter to apply the search
test_cases.expected_result: Search succeeds: the Search field displays the exact characters entered; the Courses grid filters by the literal character sequence (shows matching items or the empty-state if none match). No sanitization error or blocking modal is displayed.

test_cases.test_case: Rapid consecutive 'Star this course' actions on the same course card
preconditions: User is authenticated and on the My Courses page
Target course is visible in the current layout and is not currently Starred
steps: 1. Open the target course's card menu (three-dot menu)
2. Click 'Star this course'
3. Immediately (within one second) open the card menu again for the same course
4. Click 'Star this course' a second time
test_cases.expected_result: First click succeeds: the course is marked as Starred and is pinned to the top of the list (visible at top of grid). The second immediate attempt is blocked or treated as a no-op (menu item is disabled or shows 'Already starred'); no duplicate pin entries are created and only one card for the course remains at the top. No error banner appears.

test_cases.test_case: Remove from view then immediately verify in Hidden status filter
preconditions: User is authenticated and on the My Courses page
Target course is visible in the current layout and has status other than Hidden
steps: 1. Open the target course's card menu (three-dot menu)
2. Click 'Remove from view'
3. Open the Status_Filter dropdown
4. Select 'Hidden' from the Status_Filter dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: The 'Remove from view' action succeeds: the course is removed from the current (previous) view and is set to status Hidden. After selecting 'Hidden' in the Status_Filter, the course appears in the grid under Hidden. The course no longer appears in the previous filter (e.g., All or In progress). No duplicate entries are displayed and no error message is shown.

test_cases.test_case: Collapse all sections using the 'Collapse all' link
preconditions: Course page contains multiple expanded sections
steps: 1. Open the Course page
2. Click the 'Collapse all' link at the top right of the sections list
test_cases.expected_result: all sections collapsed

test_cases.test_case: Enable Edit mode when user is not a Student
preconditions: User has permission to edit the course (role must not be Student)
steps: 1. Open the Course page
2. Click the 'Enable Edit mode' button
test_cases.expected_result: enter edit mode

test_cases.test_case: Toggle a single section's collapsed state
preconditions: At least one section (<section>) is expanded on the Course page
steps: 1. Open the Course page
2. Click the 'Collapsed' checkbox for <section>
test_cases.expected_result: The <section> item list is hidden (section collapsed)

test_cases.test_case: Open an activity or resource by clicking its name
preconditions: Course page contains at least one section item with a clickable name
steps: 1. Open the Course page
2. Click the item name link for <activity or resource> inside a section
test_cases.expected_result: navigated to activity or resource

test_cases.test_case: Add a new section row and add an item inside it
steps: 1. Open the Course page
2. Click Add Row to add a new section
3. For the new section row, click Add Row inside its Items area to add an item
4. Enter <valid name> in the Name field for the new item
5. (Optional) Ensure the 'Collapsed' checkbox is unchecked for the new section
test_cases.expected_result: New section appears in the Sections list showing item '<valid name>'

test_cases.test_case: Student attempts to enable Edit mode (precondition violation)
preconditions: Course Page is loaded with sections
steps: 1. Login as <Student>
2. Navigate to the Course Page
3. Click the 'Enable Edit mode' button
test_cases.expected_result: Action is blocked: the page displays the constraint message 'students cannot enable Edit mode on course pages' (from the Edit_Mode_Toggle constraint) and the UI remains in view mode (no edit controls appear); course content is unchanged.

test_cases.test_case: In Edit mode, attempt to save an activity/resource with the Name field left blank (required field)
preconditions: User is authenticated as a non-Student role (e.g., <Instructor>)
Course Page contains at least one activity/resource
Edit mode can be enabled by this role
steps: 1. Login as <Instructor>
2. Navigate to the Course Page
3. Click 'Enable Edit mode'
4. Open the edit dialog for an existing activity/resource in a section
5. Clear the Name field so it is left blank
6. Click 'Save' in the activity/resource edit dialog
test_cases.expected_result: Save is blocked; the Name field displays an inline validation error indicating it is required (Name field shows required validation); the activity/resource name is not changed and the editor remains open.

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to open an activity/resource that has an empty/missing Name (required link missing)
preconditions: Course Page contains at least one activity/resource with an empty or missing Name (<blank> item)
steps: 1. Login as <Student>
2. Navigate to the Course Page that contains the item with an empty Name
3. Attempt to click the item's name/link area
test_cases.expected_result: No navigation occurs; the item is not clickable and the UI shows an inline validation indicator on the item (Name field displays an error indicating it is required); the user remains on the Course Page.

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to add a new activity/resource while leaving all required fields empty (submit-all-required-empty)
preconditions: User is authenticated as a non-Student role (e.g., <Instructor>)
Course Page has at least one section
steps: 1. Login as <Instructor>
2. Navigate to the Course Page
3. Click 'Enable Edit mode'
4. Start 'Add new activity/resource' in a section
5. Leave all required fields blank (e.g., Name left blank)
6. Click 'Save' or 'Add' to submit the new activity/resource
test_cases.expected_result: Creation is blocked; the Name field displays an inline validation error indicating it is required; no new activity/resource is created and the add dialog remains open.

test_cases.test_case: Click 'Collapse all' when there are zero Course Sections
preconditions: User is authenticated with a role allowed to view the course page
Course page is configured with zero Course Sections (repeating group is empty)
steps: 1. Navigate to the Course Page
2. Confirm there are no section entries listed in the main content area
3. Click the 'Collapse all' link
test_cases.expected_result: 'Collapse all' click succeeds; no sections are displayed before or after the action; no error message is shown and the page remains stable (UI indicates completion of the action)

test_cases.test_case: Click 'Collapse all' when some sections are already collapsed and others are expanded
preconditions: Course page contains multiple Course Sections
At least one section is pre-collapsed and at least one section is pre-expanded
steps: 1. Navigate to the Course Page
2. Verify at least one section's collapsed state is 'collapsed' and another's is 'expanded'
3. Click the 'Collapse all' link
test_cases.expected_result: 'Collapse all' click succeeds; all sections end in the collapsed state (each section's content is hidden and its collapsed indicator reflects collapsed); no error is shown

test_cases.test_case: Rapidly toggle a single section's collapsed state multiple times
preconditions: Course page contains at least one Course Section
User is on the Course Page with the section visible
steps: 1. Locate a section's collapse chevron/checkbox
2. Click the section's collapse control to collapse it
3. Immediately click the same section's collapse control to expand it
4. Immediately click the same section's collapse control again to collapse it (perform clicks in rapid succession)
test_cases.expected_result: Rapid toggle sequence succeeds; the section's final visible state equals the last click (collapsed); UI shows no error or duplicate entries and the collapsed state indicator matches the final state

test_cases.test_case: Enable Edit mode for a user who has both Student and a non-Student role (boundary of role precondition)
preconditions: User account has both Student role and at least one non-Student role (e.g., Instructor) simultaneously
User is authenticated and on the Course Page
steps: 1. Locate the 'Enable Edit mode' button on the Course Page
2. Click the 'Enable Edit mode' button
test_cases.expected_result: 'Enable Edit mode' click succeeds; the page enters edit mode and edit controls become visible (on_success 'enter edit mode' occurs); no error indicating 'students cannot enable Edit mode' is shown

test_cases.test_case: Activity/resource item Name contains long text and special/unicode characters and is clickable
preconditions: Course contains at least one section with an item whose Name includes long text (200+ characters) and special/unicode/emoji characters
User is on the Course Page
steps: 1. Locate the item link with the long/special-character Name in a section
2. Observe how the Name is rendered in the list
3. Click the item's Name link
test_cases.expected_result: Rendering and click action succeeds; the Name is displayed (may be visually truncated but visible in the UI) and clicking the link navigates to the associated activity or resource page (navigation succeeds with destination displayed); no rendering error is shown and the Name text is preserved in the link text

test_cases.test_case: Add sections then remove all sections, then use 'Collapse all'
preconditions: User can add and remove Course Sections through the UI (repeating group supports add/remove)
User is on the Course Page
steps: 1. Click to add a new Course Section
2. Add at least one Item inside the new Section (create a listed Name link)
3. Repeat add so there are multiple sections (optional)
4. Remove/delete all Course Sections until none remain
5. Click the 'Collapse all' link
test_cases.expected_result: Sequence succeeds; after removing all sections the course shows no sections, and clicking 'Collapse all' succeeds (does nothing visible) without error; UI remains stable with no sections listed

test_cases.test_case: Apply filters using the Filter Builder
steps: 1. Navigate to the Participants page
2. In the Filter Builder, choose <attribute> from the Attribute dropdown
3. Click the 'Apply filters' button
test_cases.expected_result: Participants table displays only rows matching the selected filter criteria; unrelated participants are no longer visible

test_cases.test_case: Clear filled inputs in the Filter Builder
steps: 1. Navigate to the Participants page
2. In the Filter Builder, check the <Any_Toggle>, select <attribute> in Attribute_Select and click '+ Add condition' then select <attribute> in the new condition row
3. Click the 'Clear filters' button
test_cases.expected_result: All inputs in the Filter Builder are reset to their defaults (Any toggle unchecked, Attribute dropdown unset, no condition rows present); the Filter Builder shows no active conditions

test_cases.test_case: Add a new condition row in the Filter Builder
steps: 1. Navigate to the Participants page
2. Click the '+ Add condition' link in the Filter Builder
3. In the newly added condition row, open the Attribute dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: A new condition row appears in the Conditions area with an Attribute dropdown visible for the new row

test_cases.test_case: Filter participants by First name initial using Alphabetical Filters
steps: 1. Navigate to the Participants page
2. In the Alphabetical Filters, select <selected initial> from the First_Name_Initial dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: Participants table displays only rows whose First name starts with <selected initial>; unrelated rows are no longer visible

test_cases.test_case: Filter participants by Last name initial using Alphabetical Filters
steps: 1. Navigate to the Participants page
2. In the Alphabetical Filters, select <selected initial> from the Last_Name_Initial dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: Participants table displays only rows whose Last name starts with <selected initial>; unrelated rows are no longer visible

test_cases.test_case: Navigate to a participant's profile from the participants table (Student role)
steps: 1. Navigate to the Participants page
2. Click the participant name link in the First name / Last name column for <target participant>
test_cases.expected_result: The Participant Profile page for the selected participant is displayed (navigation to participant profile); the page shows the participant's name and a Roles section

test_cases.test_case: Enrollment management actions are not available to Students
steps: 1. Navigate to the Participants page
2. Inspect the Participants page for enrollment management controls (enrol/unenrol users, edit roles, access enrollment management)
test_cases.expected_result: Enrollment management actions (enrol/unenrol users, edit roles, access enrollment management) are not present on the Participants page

test_cases.test_case: Unauthenticated user cannot access Participants page
preconditions: User is not logged in (no authenticated session)
steps: 1. As an unauthenticated user, navigate to <Participants page URL>
test_cases.expected_result: Navigation is blocked: the user is redirected to the authentication/login page (Participants list is not displayed) and no participant data or filter controls are visible.

test_cases.test_case: Student role cannot see enrollment management controls in Participants UI
steps: 1. Log in as <Student>
2. Navigate to <Participants page URL>
3. Inspect the page toolbar and a sample participant row for enrollment management controls (Enrol, Unenrol, Edit roles, link to Enrollment management)
test_cases.expected_result: Enrollment management controls (Enrol, Unenrol, Edit roles, Enrollment management link) are not present in the page toolbar or row actions for the Student user; no enrollment modal or edit-role dialog can be opened from the UI (the actions are not visible/clickable).

test_cases.test_case: Student role is blocked from accessing Enrollment Management via direct URL
steps: 1. Log in as <Student>
2. Enter <Enrollment management URL> in the browser address bar and attempt to navigate directly to it
test_cases.expected_result: Access is blocked: the Enrollment Management interface does not load for the Student user. The user either sees an access-denied/authorization error or is redirected away (for example back to Participants), and no enroll/unenrol/edit-role functionality is accessible.

test_cases.test_case: Apply filters with an added condition whose Attribute is left empty
preconditions: User is on the Participants page
steps: 1. Click the "+ Add condition" link in the Filter_Builder
2. Verify a new item appears in the Conditions repeating_group
3. Do not select any value in the new item's "Attribute" dropdown (leave it empty)
4. Click the "Apply filters" button
test_cases.expected_result: Apply filters succeeds; participants list is updated (no error shown). The empty condition item remains visible in the Filter_Builder Conditions repeating_group after Apply filters, and the participants list shows the same results as if that empty condition had been ignored

test_cases.test_case: Rapidly change First_Name_Initial selection multiple times
preconditions: User is on the Participants page
steps: 1. Open the First_Name_Initial dropdown
2. Select a letter option (e.g., choose some letter)
3. Immediately (within 1 second) select a different letter option from First_Name_Initial
4. Wait for the participants list to finish updating
test_cases.expected_result: The final First_Name_Initial selection succeeds in applying; the First_Name_Initial control displays the last-chosen letter and the participants list shows entries matching that final initial. No error dialog is shown and intermediate selections do not leave residual or duplicated filters in the UI

test_cases.test_case: Select a row checkbox, navigate to profile, then return and verify selection persistence
preconditions: User is on the Participants page
Participants_Table shows at least one participant row
steps: 1. In Participants_Table, click the selection_checkbox for a visible participant row
2. Click the participant's name link (triggering the View Profile row action) to navigate to the profile
3. From the participant profile page, use the browser back button (or the UI back navigation) to return to the Participants page
test_cases.expected_result: Navigation to the participant profile succeeds; after returning to the Participants page, the previously selected row's selection_checkbox remains checked (selection persists) and only that row is selected. No duplicate rows are selected and no selection-related error is shown

test_cases.test_case: Student role cannot see or interact with Enrollment management features
preconditions: User is logged in with role = Student
User is on the Participants page
steps: 1. Inspect the Participants page for any enrollment management UI elements (enrol/unenrol, edit roles, access enrollment management) in rows or page-level controls
2. If any enrollment management control appears visually, attempt to click it
test_cases.expected_result: Access to Enrollment_Management_Features is blocked / not visible: enrollment management controls are not present in the UI for Students. Any attempt to click a control (if it briefly appears) is blocked and does not open enrollment management; no enrol/unenrol/edit role UI is shown to the Student and no error indicating a missing permission dialog is exposed to the Student

test_cases.test_case: Toggle a course group to reveal child graded activities
preconditions: User is the owner of this Grades report
There is at least one course with graded activities listed in the report
steps: 1. Navigate to the Grades page
2. Locate the course group header for <Course Name>
3. Click the course group header to toggle collapse/expand
test_cases.expected_result: Course group header expands showing child graded activity rows beneath the header; expands_or_collapses_course_group

test_cases.test_case: AGGREGATION Course total row shows the cumulative grade across weighted items
preconditions: The student has at least one graded activity with a non-zero calculated weight
steps: 1. Navigate to the Grades page
2. Scroll to the bottom of the grade table to view the aggregation row
test_cases.expected_result: AGGREGATION Course total row is visible and displays the cumulative grade across all weighted items in the table

test_cases.test_case: Grade table columns render and ungraded items show '–' in the Grade column
preconditions: The report contains at least one graded activity with a recorded grade and one activity not yet graded
steps: 1. Navigate to the Grades page
2. Expand the course group for <Course Name> to reveal child activity rows
test_cases.expected_result: Table displays columns: Grade item, Calculated weight, Grade, Range, Percentage, Feedback, Contribution to course total; the row for <graded activity> shows a numeric value in the Grade column and the row for <ungraded activity> shows '–' in the Grade column

test_cases.test_case: Student view does not provide access to full gradebook or other students' grades
preconditions: The student does not have gradebook_access and has no permission to view other students' grades
steps: 1. Navigate to the Grades page
2. Inspect the page header and any action buttons/links around the grade table
test_cases.expected_result: No links, buttons, or controls are present to view the full gradebook or other students' grades; only this student's own grade rows and the AGGREGATION Course total row are visible

test_cases.test_case: Unauthenticated user is blocked from accessing Grades page
steps: 1. Ensure the user is logged out (no active session)
2. Navigate to the Grades page URL (attempt to open the Grades report)
test_cases.expected_result: Access is blocked: the application redirects to the Login page (login prompt is displayed) and the Grades table (including the 'AGGREGATION Course total' row) is not shown.

test_cases.test_case: Student cannot view another student's grade report (owner precondition violation)
preconditions: Logged in as Student B (a different student than the report owner)
steps: 1. Log in as <Student B> (a student who is not the owner of the target report)
2. Attempt to open the Grades report for <Student A> (navigate to the other student's Grades page or load the other student's report URL)
test_cases.expected_result: Access is blocked due to owner restriction: an authorization error banner or message is displayed indicating the user is not permitted to view this student's grades; the Grades table (all columns and the 'AGGREGATION Course total' row) is not rendered or accessible.

test_cases.test_case: Rapid consecutive Toggle Course Collapse clicks
preconditions: User is the student (owner of this report) and is logged in
Grades page contains at least one course group with child graded activity rows
steps: 1. Navigate to the Grades page
2. Identify a course header with a visible 'Toggle Course Collapse' control
3. Click the 'Toggle Course Collapse' control once
4. Immediately click the same 'Toggle Course Collapse' control again within one second
test_cases.expected_result: Rapid toggling succeeds: the course group ends in a single well-defined state (expanded or collapsed) consistent with the last click; no duplicate child rows appear and the group header's expanded/collapsed visual state updates accordingly

test_cases.test_case: Keyboard activation of course collapse (Space/Enter) accessibility edge
preconditions: User is the student (owner of this report) and is logged in
Grades page contains at least one course group with a focusable collapse control
steps: 1. Navigate to the Grades page
2. Press Tab repeatedly until the course header's collapse control receives keyboard focus
3. Press the Space key once
4. (Optional) Press Enter once on the same focused control
test_cases.expected_result: Keyboard activation succeeds: pressing Space (and Enter) toggles the course group between expanded and collapsed; the course group's child rows become visible/hidden and the header's accessible state (e.g., aria-expanded) visibly updates

test_cases.test_case: Very long Feedback text display (200+ characters)
preconditions: User is the student (owner of this report) and is logged in
Test data exists: at least one graded activity row where the Feedback field contains a very long string (>= 200 characters)
steps: 1. Navigate to the Grades page
2. Locate the graded activity row with the very long Feedback content
3. Observe the Feedback cell in the table and interact with it (hover or click) if a tooltip/expander is present
test_cases.expected_result: Long Feedback handling succeeds: the table cell does not break layout — long text is visibly truncated with an ellipsis in the cell and the full feedback is accessible via a visible mechanism (tooltip, expand control, or separate details view); there is a clear visual indicator that the content has been truncated

test_cases.test_case: Special characters and emoji in Feedback render correctly
preconditions: User is the student (owner of this report) and is logged in
Test data exists: at least one graded activity row where the Feedback contains special characters and emoji (multi-byte/unicode characters)
steps: 1. Navigate to the Grades page
2. Locate the graded activity row containing special/unicode characters in Feedback
3. Observe the Feedback cell rendering in the table and open any detail/tooltip that shows the full feedback
test_cases.expected_result: Unicode/emoji rendering succeeds: special characters and emoji are displayed correctly (not shown as placeholder boxes or garbled text); the Feedback cell and any tooltip/detail view show the characters without UI rendering errors

test_cases.test_case: High-precision Percentage and aggregation rounding behavior
preconditions: User is the student (owner of this report) and is logged in
Test data exists: at least one graded activity with Percentage stored with high decimal precision and the course has multiple weighted items affecting the aggregation row
steps: 1. Navigate to the Grades page
2. Locate the Percentage cell for the high-precision graded activity
3. Locate the 'AGGREGATION Course total' row and observe the displayed Cumulative grade
test_cases.expected_result: Precision/rounding display succeeds: the Percentage cell shows a rounded value to the UI's displayed precision (no raw extra-decimal noise), and the Aggregation Course total displays a cumulative grade whose displayed value reflects consistent rounding/aggregation; no UI overflow of decimals is shown and numeric display is stable

test_cases.test_case: Feedback that is only whitespace is trimmed in display
preconditions: User is the student (owner of this report) and is logged in
Test data exists: at least one graded activity row where the Feedback field contains only leading/trailing whitespace or is whitespace-only
steps: 1. Navigate to the Grades page
2. Locate the graded activity row with whitespace-only Feedback
3. Observe the Feedback cell in the table and any detail/tooltip view
test_cases.expected_result: Whitespace trimming succeeds: leading and trailing whitespace are not shown in the Feedback cell; the cell displays as empty or shows the standard empty placeholder (e.g., a dash) rather than visible whitespace, indicating trimming has occurred

test_cases.test_case: Open Submission Form via Add submission button
steps: 1. Click the 'Add submission' button
test_cases.expected_result: Submission form is displayed on screen (the Submission_Form panel opens showing the available input areas for this assignment)

test_cases.test_case: Submit when both Online Text and File Upload are enabled
steps: 1. Click the 'Add submission' button to open the Submission Form
2. Enter <online text> in the Online Text Editor
3. Upload a <valid file> via the File Upload Area
4. Click the 'Submit' button
test_cases.expected_result: Submission status row shows 'Submitted for grading' and the Submission summary displays an online text preview of the entered <online text> and a file entry for <uploaded file>

test_cases.test_case: Submit when only Online Text is enabled
steps: 1. Click the 'Add submission' button to open the Submission Form
2. Enter <online text> in the Online Text Editor
3. Click the 'Submit' button
test_cases.expected_result: Submission status row shows 'Submitted for grading' and the Submission summary displays an online text preview of the entered <online text>; no file attachments are listed

test_cases.test_case: Submit when only File Upload is enabled
steps: 1. Click the 'Add submission' button to open the Submission Form
2. Upload a <valid file> via the File Upload Area
3. Click the 'Submit' button
test_cases.expected_result: Submission status row shows 'Submitted for grading' and the Submission summary lists the uploaded file <uploaded file>; no online text preview is shown

test_cases.test_case: Submit when no input areas are enabled
steps: 1. Click the 'Add submission' button to open the Submission Form
2. Click the 'Submit' button without entering text or uploading files
test_cases.expected_result: Submission status row shows 'Submitted for grading' and the Submission summary shows no uploaded files and no online text preview

test_cases.test_case: Add submission action from No_Submission state opens the form
steps: 1. Click the 'Add submission' action in the Submission State Actions bar
test_cases.expected_result: Submission form is displayed (Submission_Form opens) allowing the student to enter online text or upload files as configured

test_cases.test_case: View submission from Submitted_for_grading state displays submitted content
steps: 1. Click 'View submission' in the Submission State Actions bar
test_cases.expected_result: The student's submission is displayed in a submission detail panel (shows online text preview if present and lists any uploaded files); Submission status shows 'Submitted for grading'

test_cases.test_case: Edit submission when resubmission is permitted and due date has not passed
steps: 1. Click 'Edit submission' in the Submission State Actions bar
2. Verify the Submission Form opens prefilled
3. Confirm the Online Text Editor contains the student's previous text (if applicable) and the File Upload Area shows the previously attached file(s) (if applicable)
test_cases.expected_result: Submission Form opens prefilled with the student's submission (opens Submission_Form prefilled with student's submission); previously entered online text and previously uploaded files are visible in their respective fields

test_cases.test_case: View feedback from Graded state shows grade and written feedback
steps: 1. Click 'View feedback' in the Submission State Actions bar
test_cases.expected_result: Earned grade and written feedback are displayed on the assignment page (displays earned grade and written feedback)

test_cases.test_case: View submission from Graded state shows submission and associated grade/feedback
steps: 1. Click 'View submission' in the Submission State Actions bar
test_cases.expected_result: Submission detail displays the student's submission and associated grade and written feedback (displays student's submission and associated grade/feedback)

test_cases.test_case: Attempt 'Edit submission' after the due date has passed (precondition: due date has not passed violated)
preconditions: Assignment due date is in the past (i.e., due date has passed)
steps: 1. Navigate to the Assignment page for <assignment with due date passed and entity_state == Submitted_for_grading>
2. Click the 'Edit submission' action
test_cases.expected_result: The Edit submission action is blocked: the 'Edit submission' button is not visible; Submission_Form does not open; submission remains in 'Submitted for grading' state and no edit occurs.

test_cases.test_case: Attempt 'Edit submission' when teacher does not permit resubmission (precondition violated)
preconditions: Teacher resubmission permission is set to false for this assignment
steps: 1. Navigate to the Assignment page for <assignment where teacher_permits_resubmission == false and entity_state == Submitted_for_grading>
2. Click the 'Edit submission' action
test_cases.expected_result: The Edit submission action is blocked: the 'Edit submission' button is not visible; Submission_Form does not open; submission remains in 'Submitted for grading' state and no edit occurs.

test_cases.test_case: Attempt 'View feedback' when the assignment is not graded (precondition: status must be Graded violated)
preconditions: Assignment exists with entity_state != Graded (e.g., Submitted_for_grading)
steps: 1. Navigate to the Assignment page for <assignment with entity_state != Graded>
2. Click the 'View feedback' action
test_cases.expected_result: The View feedback action is blocked: the 'View feedback' button is not visible; clicking does not display earned grade or written feedback; grading status remains unchanged (e.g., 'Not graded' or 'Submitted for grading').

test_cases.test_case: Attempt 'View submission' from No_Submission state (wrong-state action)
steps: 1. Navigate to the Assignment page for <assignment with entity_state == No_Submission>
2. Attempt to click 'View submission' (if visible)
test_cases.expected_result: The View submission action is not available: there is no 'View submission' button; the page displays 'No submissions have been made yet' (or equivalent) and no submission is shown.

test_cases.test_case: Attempt 'Add submission' while in Submitted_for_grading state (wrong-state action)
steps: 1. Navigate to the Assignment page for <assignment with entity_state == Submitted_for_grading>
2. Attempt to click the 'Add submission' button (if visible)
test_cases.expected_result: The Add submission action is not available: the 'Add submission' button is not visible (or is disabled); Submission_Form cannot be opened to create a new submission; existing submission remains unchanged.

test_cases.test_case: Attempt 'Edit submission' from Graded state (wrong-state action)
steps: 1. Navigate to the Assignment page for <assignment with entity_state == Graded>
2. Attempt to click the 'Edit submission' action (if visible)
test_cases.expected_result: The Edit submission action is not available: the 'Edit submission' button is not visible (or is disabled); Submission_Form does not open; the earned grade and written feedback remain displayed and the submission is not modified.

test_cases.test_case: Edit submission allowed when due date equals today and teacher permits resubmission
steps: 1. Open the Assignment page showing the student's submission summary
2. Click the 'Edit submission' action in the Submission status section
test_cases.expected_result: Action succeeds: Submission_Form opens prefilled with the student's previous submission. The Online Text Editor contains the previous text and any previously uploaded file is listed in the File Upload area (visible). The UI shows the form rather than an error.

test_cases.test_case: Edit submission blocked when due date has just passed (one day before today) even if teacher permits resubmission
preconditions: due_date = <one day before today> (i.e., due date has passed)
steps: 1. Open the Assignment page showing the student's submission summary
2. Click the 'Edit submission' action in the Submission status section
test_cases.expected_result: Action is blocked / error shown: The Edit submission action does not open the form. A visible inline error or toast appears indicating that editing is not permitted because the due date has passed (the Edit submission action is blocked).

test_cases.test_case: Submit when neither Online Text nor File Upload areas are enabled (no input areas)
preconditions: student is on the Assignment page in No_Submission state
steps: 1. Click the 'Add submission' button
2. On the Submission_Form (which displays no input areas), click the 'Submit' button
test_cases.expected_result: Submit succeeds: a submission is created and the Submission status summary table updates to show 'Submitted for grading'. The submission detail shows no online text and no attached files. The UI presents the 'Submitted for grading' state.

test_cases.test_case: Very long online text (200+ characters) when only Online Text is enabled
preconditions: student is creating a new submission
steps: 1. Click the 'Add submission' button
2. Enter <very long string (200+ characters)> into the Online_Text_Editor
3. Click the 'Submit' button
test_cases.expected_result: Submit succeeds: a submission is created and the submission detail displays the saved Online Text. If the UI truncates the text, the displayed content equals the truncated value (the saved detail matches what the UI displays) and no silent data loss occurs.

test_cases.test_case: Online text with leading and trailing whitespace is trimmed on save
preconditions: student is creating a new submission
steps: 1. Click the 'Add submission' button
2. Enter '  <text with leading and trailing spaces>  ' into the Online_Text_Editor (include leading and trailing spaces)
3. Click the 'Submit' button
test_cases.expected_result: Submit succeeds: a submission is created and the submission detail displays the Online Text with leading and trailing whitespace removed (trimmed). The detail page shows the trimmed value (no leading/trailing spaces).

test_cases.test_case: Upload a file whose filename contains special characters/emoji when only File Upload is enabled
preconditions: student is creating a new submission
steps: 1. Click the 'Add submission' button
2. Use the File_Upload_Area to select a file whose filename contains special characters and/or emoji (e.g., filename includes unicode symbols)
3. Click the 'Submit' button
test_cases.expected_result: Submit succeeds: a submission is created and the submission detail lists the uploaded file. The displayed filename preserves the special characters/emoji as shown in the UI and a download link for the file is present.

test_cases.test_case: Rapid re-submission after redirect: prevent duplicate submissions when using browser back
preconditions: student is starting a new submission
steps: 1. Click the 'Add submission' button
2. Fill at least one enabled input (Online_Text_Editor or File_Upload_Area) with valid content
3. Click the 'Submit' button and wait for the submission success redirect/detail page
4. Use the browser Back button to return to the Submission_Form
5. Click the 'Submit' button again without changing input
test_cases.expected_result: Second submission attempt is blocked / error shown: the system does not create a duplicate submission. The submission list or summary shows only the original single submission and the UI displays a visible message preventing duplicate submission (e.g., an inline error or toast indicating the submission is already made).

test_cases.test_case: Open an assignment from the expanded Assignments table
preconditions: Assignments section is expanded and contains an activity row
steps: 1. In the Assignments table, click the <target activity> Name link
test_cases.expected_result: navigates to activity page

test_cases.test_case: Expand Forums section and open a forum activity
preconditions: Forums section is collapsed and contains an activity row
steps: 1. Click 'Toggle Forums Section' to expand the Forums section
2. In the Forums table, click the <target activity> Name link
test_cases.expected_result: navigates to activity page

test_cases.test_case: Expand Resources section and open a resource activity
preconditions: Resources section is collapsed and contains an activity row
steps: 1. Click 'Toggle Resources Section' to expand the Resources section
2. In the Resources table, click the <target activity> Name link
test_cases.expected_result: navigates to activity page

test_cases.test_case: Expand an existing Additional Activity Type section and open its activity
preconditions: At least one Additional Activity Type section exists and is collapsed
steps: 1. Click 'Toggle Activity Type Section' on the existing Additional Activity Type to expand it
2. In the expanded Additional Activity Type table, click the <target activity> Name link
test_cases.expected_result: navigates to activity page

test_cases.test_case: Add a new Additional Activity Type row, expand it, and open its activity
steps: 1. Click 'Add Row' to create a new Additional Activity Type section
2. Click 'Toggle Activity Type Section' on the new Additional Activity Type to expand it
3. In the new Additional Activity Type table, click the <new activity> Name link
test_cases.expected_result: navigates to activity page

test_cases.test_case: Cannot click activity Name in Forums when Forums section is collapsed
preconditions: User is on the Activities page
steps: 1. Open the Activities page
2. Verify the Forums section is in the collapsed state
3. Attempt to click the activity Name link in the Forums section
test_cases.expected_result: The activity Name link in the Forums section is not visible/clickable while the section is collapsed; clicking has no effect, no navigation occurs, and the Activities page remains displayed.

test_cases.test_case: Cannot click activity Name in Resources when Resources section is collapsed
preconditions: User is on the Activities page
steps: 1. Open the Activities page
2. Verify the Resources section is in the collapsed state
3. Attempt to click the activity Name link in the Resources section
test_cases.expected_result: The activity Name link in the Resources section is not visible/clickable while the section is collapsed; clicking has no effect, no navigation occurs, and the Activities page remains displayed.

test_cases.test_case: Cannot click activity Name in an Additional Activity Type item when its section is collapsed
preconditions: User is on the Activities page
At least one Additional Activity Type exists and its Activity_Type_Section is collapsed (default)
steps: 1. Open the Activities page
2. Locate an Additional Activity Type item whose Activity_Type_Section is collapsed
3. Attempt to click the activity Name link inside that collapsed Activity_Type_Section
test_cases.expected_result: The activity Name link inside the collapsed Additional Activity Type section is not visible/clickable while the section is collapsed; clicking has no effect, no navigation occurs, and the Activities page remains displayed.

test_cases.test_case: Rapid double-click on an Assignment Name link
preconditions: User is on the Activities page
Assignments section contains at least one activity row and is expanded (default)
steps: 1. Focus the first activity row in the Assignments table
2. Click the Name link of the first activity
3. Click the same Name link again within 300ms
test_cases.expected_result: Single navigation to the activity page succeeds; the activity page is displayed. The second click does not produce a second navigation or an error message (no duplicate navigation occurs).

test_cases.test_case: Expand Forums (collapsed) and immediately open first Forum activity
preconditions: User is on the Activities page
steps: 1. Click the Toggle Forums Section control to expand Forums
2. Immediately, as the section opens, click the Name link of the first row in the Forums table
test_cases.expected_result: Navigation to the forum activity page succeeds; the activity page is displayed. If the UI defers clicks while animating, the click is queued and results in a single successful navigation; no error is shown and the Forums section expansion does not fail.

test_cases.test_case: Very long activity name with special characters and emoji displayed as link
preconditions: An activity exists (in any section) whose Name contains a very long string (>200 characters) including special characters and emoji
steps: 1. Open the Activities page
2. Locate the row whose Name contains the very long string with special characters/emoji
3. Hover the Name cell to reveal any tooltip/overflow behaviour (if present)
4. Click the Name link
test_cases.expected_result: Display handling of the long string succeeds: the table shows a truncated/ellipsized Name (or other consistent overflow UI) and, if a tooltip is provided, it reveals the full Name on hover. Clicking the Name link succeeds and navigates to the activity page; no UI rendering error or link failure is shown.

test_cases.test_case: Rapidly toggle multiple collapsible sections
preconditions: User is on the Activities page
Forums and Resources sections are present (collapsed by default)
steps: 1. Click Toggle Forums Section
2. Click Toggle Resources Section within 200ms of the previous click
3. Click Toggle Forums Section again within 200ms
4. Wait for UI to settle and inspect the visible state of both sections
test_cases.expected_result: Rapid toggle actions succeed; the final visible state of each section matches the final toggle action (Forums reflects its last toggle, Resources reflects its last toggle). No UI error messages or stuck animation occur and table contents remain accessible.

test_cases.test_case: Open Edit Profile form from User Details card
steps: 1. Click the 'Edit profile' link on the User Details card
test_cases.expected_result: opens Edit_Profile_Form — The Edit Profile form is displayed (Edit_Profile_Form); the 'General' panel is visible

test_cases.test_case: Open Data Retention Summary from Privacy and policies card
steps: 1. Click the Data Retention Summary link in the Privacy and policies card
test_cases.expected_result: opens Data_Retention_Summary — The Data Retention Summary view is displayed

test_cases.test_case: Open an enrolled course profile from the Enrolled Courses list
preconditions: Profile page is open with at least one course in Enrolled_Courses
steps: 1. Click the Course Profile link for <target course> inside the Enrolled_Courses list
test_cases.expected_result: opens course profile page — The course profile page for <target course> is displayed

test_cases.test_case: Open Blog entries from Miscellaneous card
steps: 1. Click the Blog entries link in the Miscellaneous card
test_cases.expected_result: opens Blog entries — The Blog entries view is displayed

test_cases.test_case: Open Forum posts from Miscellaneous card
steps: 1. Click the Forum posts link in the Miscellaneous card
test_cases.expected_result: opens Forum posts — The Forum posts view is displayed

test_cases.test_case: Open Forum discussions from Miscellaneous card
steps: 1. Click the Forum discussions link in the Miscellaneous card
test_cases.expected_result: opens Forum_discussions — The Forum discussions view is displayed

test_cases.test_case: Open Learning plans from Miscellaneous card
steps: 1. Click the Learning plans link in the Miscellaneous card
test_cases.expected_result: opens Learning_plans — The Learning plans view is displayed

test_cases.test_case: Open Browser sessions report from Reports card
steps: 1. Click the Browser sessions link in the Reports card
test_cases.expected_result: opens Browser_sessions — The Browser sessions report is displayed

test_cases.test_case: Open Grades overview from Reports card
steps: 1. Click the Grades overview link in the Reports card
test_cases.expected_result: opens Grades_overview — The Grades overview page is displayed

test_cases.test_case: Compose a message to the user via the Message button
steps: 1. Click the Message button in the Profile Page Actions action bar
test_cases.expected_result: opens message composer — The message composer is displayed

test_cases.test_case: Update profile: edit General fields and save
preconditions: user must be editing their own profile
steps: 1. Click the 'Edit profile' link on the User Details card to open the Edit Profile form
2. In the 'General' panel enter <First name> in First_name
3. Enter <Last name> in Last_name
4. Enter <valid email> in Email_address
5. Select <Email visibility> from Email_visibility
6. Select <Country> from Country
7. Select <Timezone> from Timezone
8. Enter <description> in Description
9. Click the 'Update profile' button
test_cases.expected_result: saves profile and returns to Profile page — The Profile page is displayed and the User Details card shows the updated name as <First name> <Last name>, Email address as <valid email>, and Timezone as <Timezone>

test_cases.test_case: Cancel profile edit and return without saving
preconditions: user must be editing their own profile
steps: 1. Click the 'Edit profile' link on the User Details card to open the Edit Profile form
2. Enter <tentative first name> in First_name
3. Enter <tentative last name> in Last_name
4. Click the 'Cancel' button
test_cases.expected_result: returns to Profile page without saving — The Profile page is displayed and the User Details card still shows the original name and other fields (the tentative changes entered are not reflected)

test_cases.test_case: Open Edit profile when not the profile owner (precondition violation)
preconditions: Viewing the Profile page of <user B> (where <user A> != <user B>)
steps: 1. On <user B>'s Profile page, locate the Edit profile link
2. Click the Edit profile link
test_cases.expected_result: Edit_Profile_Form does not open; a visible permission/authorization notification or inline error is shown on the Profile page indicating the action is not allowed; no navigation to the edit form occurs

test_cases.test_case: Submit Update profile with all required fields empty
preconditions: Logged in as the profile owner <user>
steps: 1. Clear all required fields on the Edit_Profile_Form (leave them blank)
2. Click the Update profile button
test_cases.expected_result: Form does not submit; inline validation errors appear for each required field (e.g., required fields highlighted and error indicators shown); profile is not saved; user remains on the Edit_Profile_Form

test_cases.test_case: Leave First name blank and attempt to Update profile (required text field)
preconditions: Logged in as the profile owner <user>
steps: 1. Clear the First name field (leave it blank)
2. Enter <valid last name> in Last name
3. Enter <valid email> in Email address
4. Click the Update profile button
test_cases.expected_result: First name field displays an inline validation error indicating it is required; form does not submit; profile is not saved; Edit_Profile_Form remains open

test_cases.test_case: Enter invalid format in Email address and submit
preconditions: Logged in as the profile owner <user>
steps: 1. Enter <invalid email format> in the Email address field
2. Ensure other required fields contain <valid values>
3. Click the Update profile button
test_cases.expected_result: Email address field displays an inline validation error indicating the email format is invalid; form does not submit; profile is not saved; Edit_Profile_Form remains open

test_cases.test_case: Attempt Update profile when submit precondition not met (navigate directly to edit form as non-owner)
preconditions: Logged in as <user A> who is not the profile owner
Directly navigate to the URL for Edit_Profile_Form for <user B> (bypass Edit profile link)
steps: 1. Open the Edit_Profile_Form URL for <user B> while authenticated as <user A>
2. Make any changes to form fields (or leave unchanged)
3. Click the Update profile button
test_cases.expected_result: Form submission is blocked; an authorization/permission error is displayed (e.g., error banner or modal) and the profile is not saved; user is not returned to the Profile page for <user B>; Edit_Profile_Form either remains closed or the user is redirected away without saving

test_cases.test_case: Click Cancel when precondition 'user must be editing their own profile' is not satisfied
preconditions: Logged in as <user A> who is not the profile owner
Edit_Profile_Form URL for <user B> is opened directly (if accessible) or Edit_Profile_Form button is visible due to a bug
steps: 1. On the Edit_Profile_Form for <user B> (while authenticated as <user A>), click the Cancel button
test_cases.expected_result: If Cancel is restricted by precondition, the action is blocked and a permission/authorization notification is shown; user is not allowed to exit via Cancel into editing flow for <user B>; no profile save or return-to-profile side-effect occurs. If Cancel is allowed, at minimum no unauthorized save must occur (profile remains unchanged).

test_cases.test_case: Very long Description field (>200 chars) in General panel
preconditions: User is signed in and viewing their own Profile page
steps: 1. Click the 'Edit profile' link
2. Expand the 'General' panel (if collapsed)
3. Enter a very long string (200+ characters) into the 'Description' field
4. Click the 'Update profile' button
test_cases.expected_result: Either: Form submits successfully and Profile page displays the full long Description exactly as entered (succeeds). OR: An inline validation error is shown for the Description field and the update is blocked (is blocked / error shown). The UI must show one of these visible outcomes.

test_cases.test_case: Non-Latin Unicode and emoji entered into name fields
preconditions: User is signed in and viewing their own Profile page
steps: 1. Click the 'Edit profile' link
2. Enter a string containing emoji and non-Latin Unicode characters into the 'First name' field
3. Enter a string containing emoji and non-Latin Unicode characters into the 'Last name' field
4. Click the 'Update profile' button
test_cases.expected_result: Either: Form submits successfully and the Profile page displays the First name and Last name with the entered emoji/Unicode characters (succeeds). OR: Inline validation errors are shown for the name fields and the update is blocked (is blocked / error shown). The UI must show which outcome occurred.

test_cases.test_case: Leading and trailing whitespace in Email_address and First_name is submitted
preconditions: User is signed in and viewing their own Profile page
steps: 1. Click the 'Edit profile' link
2. Enter a valid email address with leading and trailing whitespace into the 'Email address' field
3. Enter a first name with leading and trailing whitespace into the 'First name' field
4. Click the 'Update profile' button
test_cases.expected_result: Whitespace surrounding the entered values is trimmed and the form submits successfully; Profile page shows the Email address and First name without leading/trailing spaces (succeeds). If trimming is not performed, an inline validation error is shown and the update is blocked (is blocked / error shown).

test_cases.test_case: Upload a zero-byte file in New_picture_upload
preconditions: User is signed in and viewing their own Profile page
steps: 1. Click the 'Edit profile' link
2. Expand the 'User picture' panel (if collapsed)
3. Use 'New picture upload' to select and attach a zero-byte (empty) file
4. Click the 'Update profile' button
test_cases.expected_result: Either: The upload is accepted and the form submits successfully; Profile page shows the newly attached picture entry or filename (succeeds). OR: An inline file validation error is shown (e.g., file is empty) and the update is blocked (is blocked / error shown). The UI must show the acceptance or the visible error.

test_cases.test_case: Rapid re-submission after Update profile (idempotence / duplicate prevention)
preconditions: User is signed in and viewing their own Profile page
steps: 1. Click the 'Edit profile' link
2. Change the 'First name' field to a new value
3. Click the 'Update profile' button
4. After the app returns to the Profile page, immediately click the browser Back button
5. If the Edit form is shown again, click the 'Update profile' button a second time without making further changes
test_cases.expected_result: The second immediate submission does not create duplicate profiles or duplicate visible records. Either: The second submission is treated idempotently and succeeds; the Profile page shows a single profile with the updated value (succeeds). OR: The second submission is blocked by the UI (e.g., Update disabled or a visible message) and no duplicate records appear (is blocked / error shown). The UI must visibly indicate which happened.

test_cases.test_case: Click Log out terminates session and returns to Login page
steps: 1. Locate the 'Log out' button in the application header or navigation
2. Click the 'Log out' button
test_cases.expected_result: terminates the current authenticated session and redirects to the login page. The Login page is displayed with the authentication form (prompting for credentials) — the authenticated area is no longer visible.

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to use Log out when not authenticated (precondition not met)
