preconditions: User is not authenticated / no active session
steps: 1. Open the application home page (or a page that would normally show the Log out control)
2. Verify whether a 'Log out' button is present in the UI
3. Navigate directly to the logout endpoint (e.g., click a /logout link or enter the logout URL)
test_cases.expected_result: Precondition enforced: The 'Log out' control is not available to unauthenticated users (the Log out button is not visible). Navigating directly to the logout endpoint redirects to the Login page and displays the Login form; no logout success action is performed because there was no active session (session remains unauthenticated). Protected content is not accessible without authentication.

test_cases.test_case: After logging out, accessing a protected page is blocked (requires re-authentication)
steps: 1. Log in as <role>
2. Click the 'Log out' button
3. After being redirected to the Login page, attempt to navigate to a protected page URL (e.g., open <protected page URL>)
test_cases.expected_result: Logout is enforced and access is blocked: After clicking 'Log out' the user is redirected to the Login page. When attempting to open <protected page URL>, the system redirects to the Login page and does not display protected content (the Login form is shown). The session state remains Unauthenticated and protected resources require re-authentication.

test_cases.test_case: Double-click / rapid repeat of Log out button
preconditions: User is authenticated and currently viewing a protected page where the "Log out" button is visible
steps: 1. Click the "Log out" button
2. Immediately click the "Log out" button again (second click occurs before the app finishes redirecting)
test_cases.expected_result: First click succeeds: the current authenticated session is terminated and the user is redirected to the login page with the login form visible. The second click is blocked / has no additional effect: no duplicate session termination occurs, no duplicate redirects occur, and no error is shown to the user; the login page remains visible.

test_cases.test_case: Browser Back navigation after logout
preconditions: User is authenticated and on a protected page with the "Log out" button visible
steps: 1. Click the "Log out" button
2. Wait for redirect to the login page (login form visible)
3. Press the browser Back button once
test_cases.expected_result: Pressing Back is blocked from restoring protected content: the protected page is not returned to. The user remains unauthenticated and the login page (login form) is shown (either the browser stays on the login page or the protected page immediately redirects back to the login page).

test_cases.test_case: Logout in one tab, then attempt action/reload in another authenticated tab
preconditions: User is authenticated and has the same protected page open in two browser tabs (Tab A and Tab B)
steps: 1. In Tab A, click the "Log out" button
2. In Tab B, immediately reload the protected page or click a protected-action button (perform the action) without re-authenticating
test_cases.expected_result: Logout in Tab A succeeds: the session is terminated and Tab A is redirected to the login page. The attempt in Tab B is blocked / error shown in the sense that the protected request is not allowed: the reload or protected action redirects Tab B to the login page (login form visible) and the protected action does not complete under an authenticated session.

test_cases.test_case: Manually navigate to a protected URL after logout
preconditions: User is authenticated and viewing a protected page where the "Log out" button is visible
steps: 1. Click the "Log out" button
2. After redirect to the login page, enter the URL of a protected page in the browser address bar and press Enter
test_cases.expected_result: Logout succeeds: the session is terminated and the user is on the login page. The manual navigation to the protected URL is blocked: the app prevents access and redirects to the login page (login form visible). The protected page content is not accessible without re-authentication.
